Okay, welcome to module two. In a word, this module is all about intimacy. Now one of the more awkward things I've ever blurted out in front of a live audience is that copywriting is the most intimate thing you can do with your prospects with your pants still on. And while it's still a pretty lame metaphor, now that I retell it, it's still absolutely true because the greater we know our prospect, and I mean really, really know them in some ways knowing them better than they know themselves, at least within the context of the problem we're solving, than the greater our ability to successfully coach the conversion. So it won't come as any surprise that good copy starts with having a really good grasp on who you're talking to. But the truth is most marketers and business owners create this avatar or customer persona one time and then leave it on a Google Doc and never look at it again. And like any relationship, if you haven't been listening or paying attention to that person in a while, you're kind of going to forget how to relate with them. You're going to miss that nuance, that dynamic, that ever-evolving nature of who they are. What it comes out to is this. Your prospect isn't a lifeless two-dimensional persona named Tony who has 2.5 kids, lives in a Google Doc and plays golf on weekends. Your prospect is flesh and bone with a beating heart and if your copy is going to move them forward to act in any way, it's got to have a pulse too. Now legendary copywriter David Ogilvy said it best when he said the customer isn't a moron, she is your wife. And in 2018, you can assume that she's also got 20 of your friends and competitors on WhatsApp, willing to invest her time and energy with whoever makes that best attempt to understand her thoughts, her feelings, her ambitions, her failures and move her closer to her dreams. So I want you to remember this. Intimacy wins the day and this model is all about enabling that intimacy so that you can connect more deeply, build more trust and ultimately earn the privilege of creating amazing outcomes for your clients. So that's what we're going to be doing here, not only bringing your avatar to life in a vivid three-dimensional way, but also tapping into your avatar's ever-evolving nature. So remember, the version of your prospect who lands on your sales page is fundamentally different than the person who buys your product. So we're going to go through a lot of effort here to map out that transformation, what that looks like, those belief reframes, identity shifts and really pinpoint where that data and information we collect can be imprinted onto your sales page. So here's how we're going to do it. So the first part is going to be the 67 Golden Questions workbook. Now I would allocate about two hours to go through this, either on your own or with a client, but also knowing that the research you do here and all the insights you develop through these questions will serve you on every piece of your funnel, not just your sales pages, but your email sequences, your landing pages and anything else you are producing for them. So it is a bit of upfront effort. It could feel like a marathon session with a client, but it is well worth it and really sets the foundation for your messaging for all assets. Now the next part, which really moves the needle as far as getting to the core emotions of your audience, are the five advanced empathy exercises that you're going to find at the end of that workbook and in a little supplement pack I put together. So these are visualization practices that are a little more involved. They're really going to ask you to activate your imagination and walk your clients through it. But at the same time, this tends to be a really fun exercise to do with your clients or yourself. It really gets you in that mindset of who you're serving, where they're at, what they need and to explore that from all angles, not just in this two dimensional way that doesn't really give you anything to connect with. All right. Part three is going to be the coach mining and enrollment extractor. Now this is a new process that I've developed recently, working with high level coaches and consultants who routinely close anywhere between 40 to 70% over the phone. So this is going to be an opportunity to take what's working there and really try to redirect that onto a sales page. And part of the research involved here is actually re-listening to those successful enrollment conversations and extracting the messaging, extracting the patterns that they've used, extracting what their prospect was relaying as far as objections and fears and desires and all these things. So what we're really aiming for here is to retain that intimacy and exactedness and leverage it to coach the conversion at scale when it comes to our sales page. Now finally, after you've gathered all this rich data and insight, the final part will be the launch Bible, which is the synthesis. If you're a copywriter, this is a paid deliverable. You know, at the end of the day, it really is a messaging guide specific to a product or funnel that will obviously serve you if you're writing that funnel or serve your client if they wish to write it themselves. But here's where this deliverable is actually really important. It's often what I will get client sign off on and improve before going deep in writing 10,000 words between a sales page and email. So this is really where you want that initial feedback process to be. You know, once they sign off on here, they know the hoax you're going for, the arguments you're making, the beliefs you're restructuring, what the moment of highest tension is. I'm going to go through all these elements later, but you're getting approval on the core pieces of your messaging. And from there, it's really just how you're taking those pieces and arranging them systematically through your launch assets, be that your emails or your sales page. So this is an easy and quick and efficient way to really get approval, show clients you're on the same page really, and then move forward with the rest of the assets with confidence. Now, this is going to be a lot of work. Module two is where you're realistically going to spend about 50% of your time within track two. So what I want you to realize here is we are mining for diamonds. You know, be prepared to spend about a week in this module spending maybe about a day in each of the four parts I just spoke about. And this really is where the bulk of the work is. You're investing a lot of your energy up front with the tools and practices to dive deeper into your prospect than ever before. And if it feels like it's taking a long time, just trust the process. It's worth it. And remember that about 50% of your time in this program is going to be invested here. But once you've found those rich insights and synthesize them into the launch Bible, you'll be ready to coach the conversion with skill, speed, and precision. You'll no longer just be guessing and you'll no longer just stare blankly at your screen or wondering what to write next. So if you are having trouble with a blinking cursor, it's probably because you're not steeped in the problem you're solving or who you're solving it for and how that problem is showing up in their life and what the stakes and consequences are. In short, you're not active enough in the process. If you were on the phone or face to face with a potential client who is spilling their guts, you wouldn't be stuck with nothing to say. You'd probably intuitively know exactly what to say. And what we're doing here is really recreating that dynamic so that you can coach the conversion at scale. So that's what we're aiming for. We're air dropping you into a conversation, a situation that's already happening. And by the end of it, you'll be so intimately aware of your prospects problems. And so this really familiar and even bothered by their pain that you'll be desperate to solve them as if they were your own. And that's the energy of which I write all my sales pages and when you'll be prepared to after this module. So that's it. We're about to go really deep through all these avatar deep dives. So take a quick breather and when you're ready, meet me in the next lesson where you'll get started with the 67 questions workbook.
